title: lindernia plantaginea
intro: Lindernia plantaginea, also known as Creeping Lindernia, is a low-growing, herbaceous perennial plant native to tropical and subtropical regions, characterized by its small white or pink flowers and edible leaves. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Lindernia plantaginea include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and provide relief from digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Lindernia plantaginea can be used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
